items.creator: Miss Fits and the Astro Zombies
items.description: Miss Fits and the Astro Zombies December 1, 2012 Schubas Tavern Chicago, Illinois  (Covers for Cover VI) Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (38:32) 01. Rattina intro 02. London Dungeon 03. I Turned Into a Martian 04. Where Eagles Dare 05. Ghouls Night Out 06. We Are 138 07. Astro Zombies 08. Devilock 09. She 10. Hybrid Moments 11. Bullet 12. Green Hell 13. Teenagers From Mars 14. Halloween 15. Skulls 16. Last Caress Misfits cover band, performing as part of an annual all-women musicians' benefit show Covers for Cover, this year benefiting Sarah's Circle, a Chicago nonprofit serving women who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless. Other bands included Gel Set (covering Salt-N-Pepa), The Divine Hammers (covering The Breeders), Girl Group Chicago (covering girl group bands), and Lady Sentinels (covering Janet Jackson). Hosted by Rattina, one of the rat puppet hosts of the public access show Chic-a-Go-Go. Direct digital transfer Mastering: FLAC > Sound Studio (light dynamic compression, normalize, tracking) > FLAC > Tag (autofill track numbers) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Mastered by: neil d Recording 02971 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Lady Sentinels
items.description: Lady Sentinels December 1, 2012 Schubas Tavern Chicago, Illinois  (Covers for Cover VI)  Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (30:37) 01. Rattina intro 02. What Have You Done for Me Lately? 03. Escapade 04. When I Think of You 05. Nasty 06. Miss You Much 07. Rhythm Nation Janet Jackson cover band, performing as part of an annual all-women musicians' benefit show Covers for Cover, this year benefiting Sarah's Circle, a Chicago nonprofit serving women who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless. Other bands included Gel Set (covering Salt-N-Pepa), The Divine Hammers (covering The Breeders), Girl Group Chicago (covering girl group bands), and Miss Fits and the Astro Zombies (covering The Misfits). Hosted by Rattina, one of the rat puppet hosts of the public access show Chic-a-Go-Go. Everything You Paid For 10. ...And The Horse You Rode In On 11. Tear Down The Opera House Part of the Bloodshot Records 15th anniversary celebrations. items.creator: Antietam
items.description: Antietam September 29, 2000 Metro Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (44:00) 01. intro 02. New Parade 03. Here and Now 04. Big Rock 05. Blue Rose Melancholy 06. Stowaway 07. V.O.B. [Tara Key] 08. The Lush Life 09. Alibi 10. Hardly Believe Tenth-anniversary birthday party for Flower Booking, featuring Tortoise, Antietam, and the Lonesome Organist. "Here and Now," "Big Rock," and "The Lush Life" remain unreleased as of early 2026. "Stowaway" is an early version with different lyrics. Digital glitch on "Stowaway" is on original DAT recording. items.creator: Phish
items.description: Phish Friday November 9, 1990 Lounge Ax Chicago, IL 01 crowd 02 Suzy Greenberg 03 The Landlady 04 You Enjoy Myself * > 05 Possum ** 06 The Lizards *** 07 I Didn't Know 08 Llama 09 encore 10 Golgi Apparatus * Lyric changed from "God" to "Food." Two Charlie Chan signals, an Oom Pa Pa signal, and a Random Laugh signal. ** Charlie Chan and Random Note signals in intro. *** Trey forgot the words to a verse and sang it with no words instead.  Opened for Alex Chilton Tape flip after Llama Trey teased the theme from Bonanza in Suzy Greenberg. YEM's lyrics were changed from "God" to "Food." YEM contained two Charlie Chan signals, an Oom Pa Pa signal, a Random Laugh signal, and Cheap Sunglasses teases from Mike. Possum contained Charlie Chan and Random Note signals in its intro and a Dixie tease from Trey. The Lizards intro was paused for a dedication to the band's good friends Jim and Esme who were celebrating their 4th anniversary that night. Trey added The Lizards was a "song of love" and Page teased Wedding March. Trey forgot the words to a verse of The Lizards and sang it with no words instead. This show was previously uncirculated. Recording 01505 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Recorded by:  Aadam Jacobs Transferred by:  RyanJ [ryanj@ninlive.com] - www.ryanjonik.com - www.ninlive.com Mastering by: kliked Source: TEAC ME-120 + PZM (Radio Shack) > unknown mixer > Sony D5 Transfer: Master Cassette > Nakamichi CR-7A > NI Komplete Audio 6 > Adobe Audition (32/96) > WAV Mastering (Rev1): WAV > iZotope RX 11 > Reaper > Fission > xACT > flac24 Rev1 post production by Clinton Vadnais (July 2025)

items.creator: Robert Ashley
items.description: Robert Ashley September 22, 1985 Goodman Theater Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive The Electronic Language: Robert Ashley's Atalanta (Acts of God) Sept 20-22 at MCA Chicago http://robertashley.org/productions/1984-atalanta-strategy.htm A new "comic opera" in three acts by Robert Ashley, commissioned and produced by the Museum of Contemporary Art. Solo keyboards and electronics by "Blue" Gene Tyranny; percussion by Big Black (the drummer who worked with Dizzy Gillespie, not the Steve Albini group); live sound design and mixing by Paul Shorr. The Tribune did not like it https://www.chicagotribune.com/1985/09/22/ashleys-atalanta-much-less-than-meets-the-eye/ Rustling sounds at the beginning settle down after the first minute.  Tape flips/interruptions after tracks 9+14.  The script has been published but I couldn't dig up a copy, so I've divided this into unnamed tracks whenever the scene seemed to change. Length: 108:17 Source: Aiwa CM-30A Stereo Mic > Sony WM-D6C > Cassette (M) Transfer: Cassette > Yamaha K-220 > Sony A-10 (24/48) Mastering: WAV > Audacity (compress to -6dB, normalize) > FLAC (24/48) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Brian Emerick Mastered by: Brandon Recording 02108 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Depeche Mode
items.description: Depeche Mode March 22nd, 1985 Chicago, IL Aragon Ballroom Some Great Reward Tour Aadam Jacob's Collection Source: Sony WM-R2 (Internal Mics) > Cass (M) Lineage: Master cassette > Yamaha K-340 > Tascam DR-40 > WAV > Audition (normalize to -0.1db, apply fades) > Fission (track) > xACT > FLAC Taper: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Brian Emerick Mastered by: RyanJ - http://www.ryanjonik.com Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Setlist: Intro (Master And Servant Voxless) Something To Do Two Minute Warning Puppets If You Want People Are People Leave In Silence New Life Shame Somebody (tape flip) Ice Machine Lie To Me Blasphemous Rumours Told You So Master And Servant Photographic Everything Counts See You *Not Recorded* Shout Just Can't Get Enough Notes: This comes from the Aadam Jacobs live recording collection that is being digitized by a handful of people from across the globe. This is the only tape that Aadam recorded of Depeche Mode and the first recording to circulate from this date! A decent tape especially for being the Aragon which imo, is not one of the best sounding venues. Aadam wrote on the J-Card that he did not record See You. But I was pleasantly surprised to see that it indeed was. The last encore of Shout and Just Can't Get Enough were sadly not recorded.  To note from the Depeche Mode Live Wiki - because DM were not a huge draw in the States. The promoter made this show a 'Dance Festival' with local bands on the bill. As well as Henry Lee Summer, The Way Moves and Book of Love. Recording 02258 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project
items.title: Depeche Mode Live at Aragon Ballroom 1985-03-22

items.creator: Nice
items.description: Nice November 17th, 1993 Empty Bottle Chicago, IL USA Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Lineage: Master Cassette > Yamaha K-220 > Sony PCM-A10 > WAV > Audacity (trimming, normalize, fades) > CDWave (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC > mp3tag (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Brian E Mastered by: auto_pilot Total running time [19:36] ----------------------------------------- 01 talking 02 Christiana Amore 03 Dear John 04 My Perfect Fire 05 Fucked Around Nice was an Australian indie rock band which formed in 1991. The band was fronted by Randall Lee formerly of The Cannanes and later of Ashtray Boy on vocals and guitar; Susannah Stewart-Lindsay, previously a member of the Adelaide-based band, Rewind on the Paranoid Side, on guitar; and Jo Packer on drums. Francesca Bussey, later a member of The Cannanes, was a member of Nice on bass guitar during the recording sessions for their first album, Nice (1992), but left during production and was credited only as a guest. Bussey was replaced on bass guitar by Mark King. Recording 02113 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Loyola University Technology Ensemble
items.description: Loyola University Technology Ensemble Laptop Orchestra Play-along All Saints Episcopal Church Chicago, IL August 12th, 2018 Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Mastering: .WAV > iZotope RX7 [fades, normalization] > CDWav (tracking) > FLAC Frontend v2.1 (level 5) > FLAC > TagScanner 6.1.19 (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Brian E Mastered by: emmanuel Total running time [65:46] ----------------------------------------- 01 In C [Terry Riley] show info: Bring a laptop and join in with members of Loyala in a community reading of Terry Riley’s In C. This year we are part of the Thirsty Ears Festival, a street festival of classical music hosted by ACM (Access Contemporary Music). We will begin at 2pm with a brief introduction and orientation. Once we get everyone set up and making sound, we will begin our performance. A typical run lasts around 45-60 minutes. Download a copy of our app (Mac/Win) in advance or just show up and we will make sure your laptop is all set up to play along. If possible, please also bring multimedia speakers. Speakers must be connected to your laptop’s headphone jack, Bluetooth will not work. Recording 02052 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Sunshine Boys
items.description: Sunshine Boys 2018-04-28 Martyrs' Chicago IL Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Mastering: Wavelab6 > CD Wave Editor > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Mastered by: goodcooker setlist track one Oh, Caroline The Serpent in Spring Billy Boy Trippin Through Time Sideline Guys Schoolyard Bully I Was Already Gone Blue Music track ten Recording 02039 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com From Martyrs website -  The Sunshine Boys The recently launched Sunshine Boys are a Chicago-based trio  of veteran recording and touring artists who’ve served in some  of the most revered bands in indie rock, dating back to when  the term ‘indie rock’ represented a work ethic, rather than a  genre.  Featuring Freda Love Smith (Blake Babies, Antenna, Mysteries of Life) on drums,  Jacqueline Schimmel (Big Hello, Justin Roberts) on bass,  and Dag Juhlin (Poi Dog Pondering, The Slugs) on guitar,  Sunshine Boys offer a propulsive, melodic take on pop rock  that runs the range from sparkling to dark,  often within the same number.

Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist:  01 Track 01 ONO is a Chicago-based experimental music group formed in 1980. The group's music is a unique combination of experimental noise and industrial music with gospel and spoken word performance. Their lyrics directly reference themes of racism, colonialism, and homophobia (wikipedia). items.creator: Fred Lonberg-Holm's Zenith Works
items.description: Fred Lonberg-Holm's Zenith Works October 26, 2008 Hideout Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (32:44) 01. part 1 02. part 2 Part of the third annual Ears & Eyes Festival.  Zenith Works wasn't the name of one of Fred's (many) regular ensembles, so musician names are unknown, besides Fred on cello.  They played this set on the fest's closing night, before Algernon and Eleventh Dream Day. Chicago Reader: "Ears & Eyes features 21 bands spread across three nights (each show is $12), plus a prefestival party on Thursday at a secret location – to get invited to that one, you have to buy a three-day pass for $30. This is the fest’s third year, and it’s grown considerably since bassist Matthew Golombisky founded it in 2006 as a one-day party at Sylvie’s, thrown together to raise money to pay off an open-container citation received by one of his friends, who was on tour from the more public-drinking-friendly New Orleans. Golombisky, a Katrina refugee turned Chicagoan, had so much fun that he decided to make Ears & Eyes a yearly event. Since then the festival has moved from the comfy (if scuzzy) confines of Sylvie’s to Subterranean and now the Hideout." Transfer: DAT > Sony SDT-9000 > WaveDAT > WAV 16/48 Mastering: Audacity (compress to -2dB, normalize) > FLAC 16/48 Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: RyanJ [ryanj@ninlive.com] - www.ryanjonik.com - www.ninlive.com Mastered by: Brandon Recording 01665 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project

Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist:  01 These Days (Jackson Browne cover) 02 Baby Like Wine 03 Wait A Little Longer 04 Song of One 05 Uncry These Tears 06 O River 07 Good Run 08 Miss Get Back Up 09 Kalamazoo Star 10 Life's Little Ups and Downs (Charlie Rich cover) 11 Track 11 12 I Do 13 China Shop 14 Home Now Several musicians join Gerald for this last set of the day.  The 2014 Day of the Dowd was an all-day music benefit held on November 8, 2014, at Fitzgerald's Nightclub in Berwyn, IL. The event was a benefit for the Greater Chicago Food Depository and celebrated the release of Gerald Dowd's debut album, Home Now. During the event, Dowd played drums for 16 different bands over 13 hours. https://www.undergroundbee.com/2014/11/09/day-of-the-dowd Recording 01800 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project  Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Liz Phair
items.description: Liz Phair Sunday, 14 March 1999 Lounge Ax 2438 N. Lincoln Avenue  Chicago, Illinois  60614 USA Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Transfer: DAT (M) > Panasonic SV-3700 SPDIF > Marantz PMD570 Mastering: FLAC > WAV > Cool Edit Pro 2.0 (normalization; added fades at beginning and end; 16/44 conversion) > CDWav (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC > TagScanner 6.1.20 (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: John Bartol Mastered by: elegymart Total running time [1:02:21] --------------------------------------------------- 01  intro  02  Liz intro  03  Glory 04  Girls Room 05  6'1" 06  Divorce Song 07  Wild Thing 08  Johnny Feelgood 09  Dance of the Seven Veils 10  Flower 11  Mesmerizing 12  Perfect World 13  Polyester Bride 14  Go on Ahead 15  Conversation Overheard Between Two Bouncers 16  Fuck and Run 17  Explain It to Me 18  Supernova 19  MC / Liz comments Band line-up: Liz Phair - vocals, guitar Buddy Judge - electric guitar Katie Brown - harmony vocals on t10 Notes: Sally Timms opened. Followed by a Hot Damn fashion show (by Liz's sister-in-law, Sarah Staskauskas). Spinning by DJ U-Gene. Show was not publicized, unlisted on Lounge Ax website, and not handled by Liz's management. Tickets were $15, sold at the Lounge Ax bar, and limited to two per customer.  Recording 00296 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Velour Motel
items.description: Velour Motel September 13th, 1997 Mike's Moonlite Luv Cruise The Jamaica Chicago River Chicago, IL USA Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Transfer: Panasonic SV-3700 SPDIF --> Marantz PMD570 Extra space trimmed off in Pro Tools Mastering: .wav > Audacity (trimming, normalize, fades) > CDWave (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC > mp3tag (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: John B. Mastered by: auto_pilot Total running time [41:25] ----------------------------------------- 01 Thax Douglas poem (start cut) 02 unknown 03 unknown 04 unknown 05 unknown 06 MC 07 Gold 08 talking 09 The Wreck of the Edmund Fitzgerald [Gordon Lightfoot] 10 unknown (cut) https://chicagoreader.com/arts-culture/days-of-the-week-98/ 13 SATURDAY Mike’s Moonlite Luv Cruise is named after Velour Motel member Mike Armstrong, who’s a seafaring man by day. He’s hoping for a minimum of seasickness and Charo-esque stowaways on tonight’s four-hour excursion aboard the Jamaica. Velour Motel will be joined by other mellow hipster bands–the Aluminum Group, Ashtray Boy, the Pulsars, and the Handsome Family–and organizers point out that if people don’t like one of the bands on the bill they can go up to (or jump off of) the bridge to avoid the noise. Doors open at 6:30, and the vessel sets sail at 7 from 220 W. Lower Wacker, between Franklin and Wells. The $26 ticket includes all the music and fresh air you can take and a free buffet. Tickets are available at Reckless Records and must be bought in advance; call 773-489-1656 for more. Recording 00287 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project
items.title: Velour Motel Live at Mike'a Moonlite Luv Cruise 1997-09-13

items.creator: The Aluminum Group
items.description: The Aluminum Group September 13th, 1997 Mike's Moonlite Luv Cruise The Jamaica Chicago River Chicago, IL USA Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Transfer: Panasonic SV-3700 SPDIF --> Marantz PMD570 Extra space trimmed off in Pro Tools Mastering: .wav > Audacity (trimming, normalize, fades) > CDWave (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC > mp3tag (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: John B. Mastered by: auto_pilot Total running time [48:37] ----------------------------------------- 01 The Mattachine Society (end only) 02 I Can See It 03 Angel On A Trampoline 04 Sunday Morning 05 Pretty Mouth and Green My Eyes 06 House Emptied Into Hole 07 keyboard interlude whilst exiting lock 08 Chocolates 09 Jinxed 10 Loverly (aka Wouldn't It Be Loverly) 11 Storytime https://chicagoreader.com/arts-culture/days-of-the-week-98/ 13 SATURDAY Mike’s Moonlite Luv Cruise is named after Velour Motel member Mike Armstrong, who’s a seafaring man by day. He’s hoping for a minimum of seasickness and Charo-esque stowaways on tonight’s four-hour excursion aboard the Jamaica. Velour Motel will be joined by other mellow hipster bands–the Aluminum Group, Ashtray Boy, the Pulsars, and the Handsome Family–and organizers point out that if people don’t like one of the bands on the bill they can go up to (or jump off of) the bridge to avoid the noise. Doors open at 6:30, and the vessel sets sail at 7 from 220 W. Lower Wacker, between Franklin and Wells. The $26 ticket includes all the music and fresh air you can take and a free buffet. Tickets are available at Reckless Records and must be bought in advance; call 773-489-1656 for more. As per between-song comments, The Aluminum Group set took place whilst The Jamaica was transiting the Chicago River Locks to Lake Michigan. Recording 00087 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project

items.description: Moonshine Willy Empty Bottle February 21, 1998 Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: 01 intro 02 Burn in Hell 03 Learning Song 04 Bastard Child 05 Turn the Lights Down Low 06 Dig a Little Deeper 07 Goin' Down 08 Always 09 Goin' Along for the Ride 10 Passing Affair 11 Nobody Wants to Die 12 Don't You Want Me The very first band signed to Bloodshot Records, Moonshine Willy played this show just a few weeks before the release of their third and final album Bastard Child, which all these songs appear on. Western Avenue Chicago, Illinois  60622 USA Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Transfer: DAT > Sony SDT-9000 > WaveDAT > WAV (16bit/48kHz) Mastering: FLAC > WAV > Cool Edit Pro 2.0 (normalization; added fades at beginning and end; convert to 16/44) > CDWav (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC > TagScanner 6.1.20 (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: RyanJ [ryanj@ninlive.com] - www.ryanjonik.com - www.ninlive.com Mastered by: elegymart Total running time  [1:09:17] ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 01  intro  02  unknown 03  unknown 04  Rakim - Addictive  05  unknown  06  unknown  07  Flying Lotus - Sangria Spin Cycles 08  unknown  09  unknown  10  unknown  11  unknown  12  unknown  13  Busta Rhymes - What Up (prod J Dilla) 14  unknown  15  Flying Lotus - Massage Situation 16  unknown  17  unknown 18  unknown  19  Justin Timberlake & Timbaland - Ayo Technology 20  unknown  21  unknown  22  unknown  23  Larry Levan - Is It All Over My Face? (Female Vocal Version) 24  Taz Arnold (aka TI$A) - Vote Obama 25  closing comments Band line-up: Steven Ellison (aka Flying Lotus) - laptop, mixers Notes: Sounds like there are sound issues for this gig. FlyLo comes through clear, but the music is somewhat distorted and low. He requests that they take the compressors off after t06 which only provides some minor improvement. Aadam's calendar notes that he left during the encore. Recording 01683 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Algernon
items.description: Algernon October 26, 2008 Hideout - Ears and Eyes Festival Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Transfer: DAT > Sony SDT-9000 > WaveDAT > WAV (16bit/48kHz) Mastering: .FLAC > FFmpeg > .WAV > Adobe Audition 2025 [normalize, fades] > CDWav [tracking] > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .FLAC > MP3tag v3.28 [tagging]. Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: RyanJ [ryanj@ninlive.com] - www.ryanjonik.com - www.ninlive.com Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist:  01 [introduction] 02 Track 02 03 Track 03 04 Debrief and Defect 05 Four Four Four 06 Objective Compromised "ALGERNON were formed in early 2000's in Chicago. They're describing themselves (as on their MySpace page)as a blend of neo-psychedelia, jazz and post-rock.Indeed they have a post-rock esaence, but their sonic landscapes are meandering everywhare, with a particular nod towards jazz - which is not surprising since two of their guitarista are coming from the jazz-palette of contemporary scene, and 'Chicago Jazz Magazine' boldly states Dave Miller and Nick Fryer are the best among younger ones. They're supported by rhythm-section which comprises of Tom Perona on bass and Cory Healey on drums. Katie Wiegman on vibes completes the picture. The band's line-up varied through its evolution: former members are Past members/contributors: Jeff Greene and Hyosub "Fast Eddie" Kim on bass, Nick Fryer and Tim Venchus on guitar, Jess Birch and Jamie Gallagher on drums and Darren Garvey on both vibraphone and drums." (source:https://www.progarchives.com/artist.asp?id=5228) Recording 01666 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project  Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: The Peelers
items.description: The Peelers April 27, 2003 Abbey Pub Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Transfer: DAT > Sony SDT-9000 > WaveDAT > WAV (16bit/48kHz) Mastering: .FLAC > FFmpeg > .WAV > Adobe Audition 2025 [normalize, fades] > CDWav [tracking] > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .FLAC > MP3tag v3.28 [tagging]. Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: RyanJ [ryanj@ninlive.com] - www.ryanjonik.com - www.ninlive.com Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist:  01 Track 01 02 Track 02 03 The Motion 04 Junkie 05 Rockin on Your Dime 06 Take a Ride 07 The Way I Feel 08 Falling Down 09 Want Some 10 That's Why I'm Leaving "Formed in early 2000, The Peelers play really fuckin' loud and really fuckin' hard. they have built a reputation as one of the most energetic, exciting and sometimes bloody live bands in Chicago. They have shaken asses and foundations around the mid-west with bands such as Sons Of Herculies, Supersuckers, Lazy Cowgirls, Baseball Furies, Cash Audio and their drummers former band Gaza Strippers. In the summer of 2002 the band went into Engine Studios in Chicago, and recorded their debut single and released it on their own. the single has had great reviews, Chicago's NEW CITY readers poll 2002 voted them Chicago's best new band. THEGETDOWNSYNDROME was recorded by Texas legend Tim Kerr (Mooney Suzuki, Mono Men, Quadrajets, Sugar Shack, Motards, just to name a few)... The boys plan to spend a lot of time on the road in 2004... ARE YOU READY?" - (http://www.orangerecordings.com/peelers.html) Recording 01688 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project  Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project
items.title: The Peelers Live at Abbey Pub 2003-04-27

items.creator: Phish
items.description: Phish 1990-11-09 Lounge Ax Chicago, IL Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Recorded by:  Aadam Jacobs Transferred by:  RyanJ [ryanj@ninlive.com] - www.ryanjonik.com - www.ninlive.com Mastering by: kliked Source: TEAC ME-120 + PZM (Radio Shack) > unknown mixer > Sony D5 Transfer: Master Cassette > Nakamichi CR-7A > NI Komplete Audio 6 > Adobe Audition (32/96) > WAV Mastering: WAV > Adobe Audition/Izotope 9 (seperate tracks, compression & EQ) > Audacity (tracking) > FLAC > MP3Tag Setlist: (1:00:14) 1. Suzy Greenberg 2. The Landlady 3. You Enjoy Myself* > 4. Possum** 5. The Lizards*** 6. I Didn't Know 7. Llama (tape flip) 8. Golgi Apparatus *Lyric changed from "God" to "Food." Two Charlie Chan signals, an Oom Pa Pa signal, and a Random Laugh signal. **Charlie Chan and Random Note signals in intro. ***Trey forgot the words to a verse and sang it with no words instead.  Trey teased the theme from Bonanza in Suzy Greenberg. YEM's lyrics were changed from "God" to "Food." YEM contained two Charlie Chan signals, an Oom Pa Pa signal, a Random Laugh signal, and Cheap Sunglasses teases from Mike. Possum contained Charlie Chan and Random Note signals in its intro and a Dixie tease from Trey. The Lizards intro was paused for a dedication to the band's good friends Jim and Esme who were celebrating their 4th anniversary that night. Trey added The Lizards was a "song of love" and Page teased Wedding March. Trey forgot the words to a verse of The Lizards and sang it with no words instead. Opening for Alex Chilton This show was previously uncirculated. Recording 01505 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project
items.title: Phish Live at Lounge Ax on 1990-11-09

items.creator: Negligents
items.description: Negligents Metro January 14, 2006 Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: 01 intro 02 Caramel Well 03 Worms 04 Nerves [Silkworm] 05 Turn Up the Suck Knob 06 Friends of Friends 07 Call Off the Spaceships 08 unknown ("frustrated man") 09 Do the Take Away 10 unknown ("want to take a chance") 11 Trouble Opening for Exo, the Dials, and the Returnables. The Negligents were a Chicago-based band fronted by Matt Crawford that recorded two albums, 2004’s “Our Love Is Not Weird” and 2009’s “Good Advice,” even if Discogs doesn’t recognize them as the same band. Crawford is the brother of Rebecca Crawford of the Dials, who was married to guitarist John Glick of the Returnables in 2005 when Glick was killed, along with Dials drummer Doug Meis and Silkworm drummer Michael Dahlquist, when their car was rammed at a stoplight in Chicago by a car driven by a woman trying to die by suicide. This show was a memorial for the three musicians, and each band that performed covered one Silkworm song. Mastering: FLAC > iZotope RX 11 (de-click to remove close clapping) > FLAC > Sound Studio (adjust levels, very light dynamic compression, normalize, tracking) > WAV > xACT (generate ffp) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Ryan J Mastered by: neil d Recording 01550 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project

items.creator: Roky Erickson & The Explosives
items.description: ROKY ERICKSON & THE EXPLOSIVES Saturday, 24 June 2006 Union Park  Virtue Stage 1501 W. Randolph Street Chicago, Illinois  60607 Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Mastering: .WAV > Cool Edit Pro 2.0 (normalization; added fades at beginning and end) > CDWav (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC >  TagScanner 6.1.19 (tagging) Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: elegymart Total running time [1:03:08] ---------------------------------------------------------------- 01  introduction 02  Thax Douglas poem/intro 03  Cold Night for Alligators 04  White Faces 05  Don't Shake Me Lucifer 06  The Interpreter 07  The Beast 08  Bermuda 09  Splash 1 10  Creature with the Atom Brain 11  Starry Eyes 12  I Think of Demons 13  Two Headed Dog (Red Temple Prayer) 14  You're Gonna Miss Me 15  I Walked with a Zombie 16  Before You Accuse Me It's a Cold Night for Alligators A. " 'The Interpreter,'  A. "We're doing 'Splash 1' and 'I Had to Tell You.' " White Faces Band line-up: Roky Erickson – lead vocals, rhythm and lead guitar Cam King - lead and rhythm guitar, backing vocals Chris Johnson - bass, backing vocals Freddie Steady Krc - drums, backing vocals Notes: Second year of the Intonation Festival. This was the first night of the weekend festival. Other acts on this day included Favourite Sons, Erase Errata, 90 Day Men, Devin the Dude, Jose Gonzalez, Chromeo, High on Fire, Stills, Boredoms, Ghostface Killah, Lady Sovereign, and Streets. Recording 01366 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.description: Jelly Bishops May 9, 1986 West End Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (26:49) 01. soundcheck and intro 02. Hard to Be Human (drum machine issues) 03. Hard to Be Human 04. Before I Grow Too Old [Fats Domino] 05. 20th Century Boy 06. Lost Highway 07. Where Were You 08. Armalite Rifle [Gang of Four] Jon Langford and John Brennan of the Three Johns and Tom Greenhalgh of the Mekons (accompanied by Hugo, the Three Johns' drum machine) combined to form the Jelly Bishops after John Hyatt of the Three Johns went home early from a U.S. tour, performing a mix of Mekons songs and covers. items.creator: Sally Timms
items.description: Sally Timms and Fred Armisen May 15, 1997 Lounge Ax Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (13:33) 01. intro 02. Você Tem [Paulinho e Beatriz] 03. unreleased original ("nossa senhora") 04. unreleased original ("children don't swim in deep water") 05. Waterfalls [TLC] A short set by Sally Timms and her then-boyfriend Fred Armisen performing as "Saru Mimi," as part of opening night of Lounge Ax's “Viva Godzilla: The Show That Ate Chicago,” a combination art exhibit and rock show that also included Mount Shasta, Cash Money, Dianogah, and a new band fronted by Rian Murphy. "Você Tem" is a song that Armisen later performed with Maya Rudolph in a Saturday Night Live sketch, then recorded with her as the fictional Brazilian band "Paulinho e Beatriz"; the next two songs, one in Portuguese and the other in mixed Spanish and English, are unreleased Armisen/Timms originals. Mastering: FLAC > Sound Studio (light dynamic compression, normalizing) > WAV > xACT (generate ffp) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastering by: neil d Recording 01288 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Live concert
Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project
items.title: Sally Timms and Fred Armisen Live at Lounge Ax on 1997-05-15

items.description: Cheer-Accident April 18, 2010 Bottom Lounge Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (72:38) 01. soundcheck & intro 02. Trial of Error 03. I Feel Sick 04. Sleep 05. Blue Cheadle 06. Empty Province 07. Provincial Din 08. All Over 09. According to the Spiral 10. Facialization 11. Drag You Down 12. The Day After I Never Met You 13. Theme from Shaft 14. Big Secret Airshow* Chicago Reader: "Since last year’s Fear Draws Misfortune (Cuneiform) they’ve also got a new lineup, with keyboardist D Bayne and singer Carmen Armillas — the first permanent front person in the band’s three-decade history — joining Jones and regulars Jeff Libersher, Alex Perkolup, and Andrea Faught." Opening for Sleepytime Gorilla Museum.  "Big Secret Airshow" is not the song from their 1993 album but a prank featuring guest Gabriel "Gabe Viles" Williams of the group Mahjongg.  A video of this performance was released on the band's youtube - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qfOe1o4_Vpw Mastering: Audacity (compress to -4.5dB, normalize) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastered by: Brandon Recording 00600 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Robyn Hitchcock
items.description: Robyn Hitchcock July 18, 1999 The Quaker Goes Deaf Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (35:16) 01. intro 02. Silver Dagger 03. Sleeping With Your Devil Mask 04. Oceanside 05. De Chirico Street 06. I Wanna Destroy You 07. Tonight 08. Mexican God 09. Cynthia Mask Robyn played an outdoor daytime set between his two evening gigs at the Metro, a couple days before the release of "Jewels for Sophia."  Some mic difficulties, so sometimes he is singing off-mic.  The performance includes a unique improvised version of "I Wanna Destroy You," and the live debut of "Mexican God."  The last song is cut off. Mastering: iZotope RX (declick+lower applause) > Audacity (compress to -5dB, normalize) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastered by: Brandon Recording 01248 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.description: Kelly Hogan, Nora O'Connor, Scott Ligon October 5, 2005 Cultural Center Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (13:46) 01. Nora intro 02. A Taste of Honey [Lenny Welch] 03. One Bad Apple [The Osmonds] 04. Kelly intro 05. I Want a Little Sugar In My Bowl [Nina Simone] 06. Ole Buttermilk Sky [Hoagy Carmichael] 07. Don't Sell Hot Dogs Tonight [The Leather Uppers] This is two short segments of Thomas Dunning's Hoot Night at the Chicago Cultural Center.  The theme of the night was food, and other performers included Deanna Varagona, Dag Juhlin, Eric Ziegenhagen, Zapruder Point, Joie de Diva, Steph Turner, Michael Ross, Thomas Dunning & Your Boyfriends, each group performing just a couple songs on the food theme.  Kelly joins Nora on "One Bad Apple," and both of them are backed by Scott Ligon. items.creator: Mekons
items.description: Mekons June 14, 1992 Lounge Ax Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (94:36) 01. intro 02. Memphis, Egypt 03. Only Darkness Has the Power 04. Millionaire 05. Sorcerer 06. Club Mekon 07. All I Want 08. Ghosts of American Astronauts 09. Lyric 10. Wicked Midnite 11. The Curse 12. Blow Your Tuneless Trumpet 13. Amnesia 14. 100% Song 15. Wild and Blue 16. Last Dance 17. Big Zombie 18. Empire of the Senseless 19. Hard to Be Human Jon Langford - vocals, guitar Tom Greenhalgh - vocals, guitar Sally Timms - vocals Susie Honeyman - violin Sarah Corina - bass Jon Langley - drums "We are the Nelsons!" announces Jon Langford at the top of the set, honoring Chicago DJ Terry Nelson in what becomes a running joke throughout the show. (Songs are later introduced as "Club Nelson," "Empire of the Nelsons," etc.) One of the best recordings from the 1992 U.S. tour, featuring lots of songs from "Rock 'n' Roll" and "Curse of the Mekons" and a few sneak peaks ahead at "I [Heart] Mekons."  Mastering: FLAC > Sound Studio (very light dynamic compression, normalizing) > WAV > xACT (generate ffp) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastering by: neil d Recording 01175 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Mekons
items.description: Mekons (as Where Were U2) October 10, 1999 Schubas Tavern Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Mastering: .FLAC > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .WAV > Adobe Audition 2025 [fades] > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .FLAC > MP3tag v3.28 [tagging]. Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist:  01 (I'm Not Here) 1967 02 Gin Palace 03 Lost Highway (Hank Williams) 04 Chopper Squad 05 When Darkness Falls 06 After Six 07 The Letter 08 Fancy (The Kinks) 09 Now We Have The Bomb 10 Orpheus 11 Betrayal 12 The Ballad Of Sally 13 Point Of No Return Mekons play support to Johny Down as being a Australian Mekons coverband with the name Where Were U2. Source: www.mekons.de/99shows.htm October 10th: Mekons Tribute band opens for Johnny Dowd at Schuba's in Chicago Caught a pretty cool cover band last night at Shuba's opening for Johnny Dowd... They were called Where Were U2, an Australian tribute to the Mekons. A fat guy on guitar and vocals said he was the Sally Timms figure in the band because lots of men kept trying to pick him up and he always ended up sleeping with 2 or 3 of them that night. At times they sounded remarkably like the Mekons and even covered material from Hen's Teeth like "The Ballad of Sally", and "Fancy" which the Mekons themselves don't even play. They must of not been able to find a female bass player to fill in for Sarah because a guy (I believe from the Pulsars, but I could be wrong) was on bass. Obviously it was impossible to have anyone imitate the sounds Lu makes because there was no one playing the string things he plays.On the whole the show was fun but short (60min).  Avery Lerner This was one of the best 'kons sets I've caught (& there've been quite a few)--Jon's sense of sarcastic humor was firmly in place (with his tongue in his cheeks), the band was relaxed and wonderfully sloppy. The set (the list of which I deliberately forgot to make) included a whole bunch of stuff they don't usually know how to play. The Where Were U2 show last night was a very memorable show indeed. It started as a low key night with it being Sunday and the Mekons being the opener, but they dug into the archives and played some nuggets that I haven't heard played live for the last few years or even longer. I remember "I'm Not Here," "Chopper Squad," and "Lost Highway" as being among the surprises. Lonesome Bob (announced as Crocodile Dundee by Jon) showed up and tore the house down with a great version of "Point of No Return." As was mentioned, Langford came up with another entertaining shtick with the Australlian cover band theme. The mythical tribute band's reasons for liking the Mekons: they can't really play; they're lefties; and....they're drunk all the time. Greg Kot was there taking notes, so perhaps tomorrow's Chicago Tribune will have a write-up. That Johnny Dowd is one scarry dude. Jim M. Recording 00760 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project  Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: WLUW Holiday Sing-A-Long
items.description: WLUW Holiday Sing-A-Long December 17, 2003 Schubas Tavern Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Mastering: .FLAC > Adobe Audition 2025 [append, normalize, edits, fades] > .WAV > CDWav [tracking] > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .FLAC > MP3tag v3.28 [tagging]. Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist:  01 Introduction (Elizabeth Conant) 02 Silver Bells (Elizatbeth Conant and Thax Douglas) 03 Christmas Time Is Here (Charles Kim & Grand Lunar) 04 Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy (Charles Kim & Grand Lunar) 05 The Chipmunk Song (Charles Kim & Grand Lunar) 06 Rudolph The Red-Nosed Reindeer (Elizabeth Conant) 07 Blue Christmas (Elizabeth Conant) 08 God Rest Ye Merry Gentlemen (Elizabeth Conant) 09 O Little Town of Bethlehem (Elizabeth Conant) 10 Five Foot Two, Eyes of Blue (snippet) (Elizabeth Conant) 11 The Christmas Song (Merry Christmas to You) (snippet) (Elizabeth Conant) 12 Christmas (Baby Please Come Home) (The M's) 13 King of the Elves (The M's) 14 People That You Know (The M's) 15 Merry Christmas,  Happy Monday (The M's) 16 [talks] (Elizabeth Conant) 17 Jingle Bells  (Elizabeth Conant) 18 Frosty The Snowman (Elizabeth Conant) 19 We Wish You a Merry Christmas (Elizabeth Conant) 20 Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas (Elizabeth Conant) 21 First Snowfall (instrumental) (The Coctails) 22 instrumental (The Coctails) 23 Holiday in Paradise (instrumental) (The Coctails) 24 White Christmas (Kelly Hogan and The Coctails) 25 Why (Dirty Wurds cover) 26 [talks] (Elizabeth Conant and Shawn Campbell) 27 Greensleeves (Elizabeth Conant) 28 Christmas Time is Here (Elizabeth Conant) 29 Comes n Goes (Steve Frisbie and Liam Davis) 30 Jesus Christ (Big Star cover) 31 Jesus (Velvet Underground cover) (Big Star cover) 32 There's No Christmas Without You (Kirk Franklin cover) (Big Star cover) 33 O Holy Night (Big Star cover) 34 [tuning] (Detholz) 35 Blood On The Snow (poem) (Detholz) 36 Santa Claus Is Coming To Town (Detholz) 37 Silver and Gold (Detholz) 38 Frosty The Snowman (Detholz) 39 Celebration (Detholz)  Show is featuring Steve Frisbie and Liam Davis (of Frisbie). Mark Greenburg and John Upchurch (formerly of The Coctails). Kelly Hogan. The M's, The Detholz! and Charles Kim. "The Detholz!, Chicago’s “self-appointed herald of the last days,” will change their tune at the WLUW Holiday Sing-Along. Also playing the third annual fund-raiser: the M’s, ex-Coctails John Upchurch and Mark Greenberg, Steve Frisbie and Liam Davis of Frisbie, and Charles Kim & Grand Lunar, all performing new and classic holiday music. Local chanteuse and sometime WLUW DJ Elizabeth Conant leads the caroling between sets. It’s tonight at 8:30 at Schubas, 3159 N. Southport in Chicago. Tickets are $10, and you must be 18. Call 773-525-2508; for more info on the radio station log on to www.wluw.org." Recording 01024 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project  Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: The Coctails
items.description: The Coctails September 26, 2008 Hideout Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (58:53) 01. Tim Tuten intro 02. First Snowfall 03. Skeleton Bones 04. The Penguin, Powerhouse 05. Martin Denny's Sake Rock 06. Wood Bee 07. Don't Got Time 08. 2000 09. Wheels 10. Miss Maple 11. Weather King 12. Walkin' Down the Street 13. Talkin' 'Bout My Baby 14. Why The Coctails reunited for this 16th anniversary show for Carrot Top Records, celebrating label founder Patrick Monaghan ("is he crying? is he crying? somebody kick him") along with The Bitter Tears, KatJon Band, and Antietam (and more bands the following night). The show also served as a stealth release party for the "Happy Sweet Sixteen Carrot Top Records" CDR compilation. items.creator: The Minus 5
items.description: The Minus 5 and Wilco January 09, 2000 Lounge Ax Chicago, Illinois Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Mastering: .FLAC (in separate tracks) > Adobe Audition 2025 [append tracks, normalize] > .WAV > Izotope RX 11 [edits, fades] > CDWav [tracking] > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .FLAC > MP3tag v3.28 [tagging]. Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist: 01 Soundcheck 02 Courage Is The Smallest Bird (partial, stops to wait for Kelly Hogan) 03 Courage Is The Smallest Bird 04 In A Lonely Coffin 05 I'm Not Bitter  06 Dear Employer (The Reason I Quit) 07 Lies Of The Living Dead 08 The King Of Carrot Flowers, Pt. One (Neutral Milk Hotel) 09 Disaster Nurse Fan 10. Lyrical Stance 11. The Night Chicago Died Again (The Death of Minus 5) 12. Last Call For Corporal Barry 13. I Still Miss Someone (Johnny Cash) 14. Home Of The Blues (Johnny Cash) 15. I Wish I Was Your Mother (Mott The Hoople) On January 9, 2000, The Minus 5 played a secret show at Lounge Ax in Chicago, opening for Wilco (billed as "Summer Teeth") for 300 lucky fans. This was a farewell gig as the beloved Lincoln Park venue would shut its doors just one week later. For this gig, the Minus 5 was just McCaughey fronting Wilco, with frontman Jeff Tweedy playing bass. The group raced through a handful of brand-new, unrehearsed songs alongside covers of influences as diverse as Mott The Hoople, Johnny Cash and Neutral Milk Hotel (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Minus_5) Info and images at https://meetingmyheroes.substack.com/p/wilco-and-lounge-ax Recording 00767 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project  Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com
subject: Aadam Jacobs
AJC Project

items.creator: Mekons
items.description: Mekons July 10, 2015 Square Roots Festival, Old Town School of Folk Music, Chicago Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive ** 16 BIT ** Mastering: WAV > iZotope RX9 (de-click to reduce close clapping) > Sound Studio (light dynamic compression, normalize, tracking) > WAV > xACT (fix SBEs, generate ffp) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: neil d Setlist:  01 intro 02 Memphis, Egypt 03 Beaten and Broken 04 Tina 05 Millionaire 06 Diamonds 07 Fantastic Voyage 08 (Sometimes I Feel Like) Fletcher Christian 09 Orpheus 10 Now We Have the Bomb 11 Last Dance 12 The Curse 13 Hard to Be Human 14 Afar and Forlorn 15 Thee Olde Trip to Jerusalem 16 Shanty - Wild and Blue 17 Big Zombie Eric Bellis - vocals, accordion Lu Edmonds - saz, vocals Steve Goulding - drums Tom Greenhalgh - vocals, guitar Susie Honeyman - violin Jon Langford - vocals, guitar Sally Timms - vocals Dave Trumfio - bass From Jon Langford's tour diary in Counterpunch: Mekons have not played in our full eight-piece glory for over 3 years. We have no idea why. Life often gets in the way of the punk rockin’ when you’ve been at it for 35+ trail blazing years burrowing on the fringes of unpopular culture. So, why now? Just this vague feeling that we should probably get together and this July everyone is miraculously willing and available. So we decide to play a bunch of gigs in and around Chicago, then jump in a van and head east to play more gigs and record a new album (skillfully writing all the songs in aforementioned van as we hurtle down interstates in various eastern states and states of hungover decrepitude and semi-consciousness). Recording #0322 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: The Flat Five
items.description: The Flat Five April 24, 2014 Hideout Chicago IL Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (134:25) 01. Man Without a Heart [The Hollies] 02. What Kind of Girl Are You [The Hollies] 03. Love Lots of Lovin' [Lee Dorsey] 04. Kiss Me Baby [Beach Boys] 05. Without Rhyme or Reason [Spanky and Our Gang] 06. unknown title (doo doo doo) 07. Birds of a Feather [Joe South] 08. Mama Don't Like My Man [Sharon Jones] 09. Kites Are Fun [The Free Design] 10. Niki [The Third Wave] 11. Lil Darlin' [Hefti & Hendricks] 12. The Raven 13. Crying in the Rain [Carole King] 14. Don't Ever Change [Carole King] 15. Just Once In My Life [Carole King] 16. Pleasant Valley Sunday [Carole King] 17. This Is Your Night 18. This Is Your Night 19. The Camping Song 20. Blue Prelude [Nina Simone] 21. Little Bell [The Dixie Cups] 22. I Push Right Over [Robbie Fulks] 23. Tomorrow Won't Bring the Rain [Dion] 24. Almond Grove 25. Florida 26. Free Man in Paris [Joni Mitchell] 27. She's a Big Girl Now [Dickey Lee] 28. Ain't Nothing Like the Real Thing [Marvin Gaye & Tammi Terrell] 29. Friends [Beach Boys] 30. Fightin' Back 31. The Animal Life 32. Love Is Only Sleeping [The Monkees] 33. No True Love [The Dixie Cups] 34. Lazybones [Hoagy Carmichael & Johnny Mercer] 35. The Party [Henry Mancini] AKA The Flat Five Chicago: Kelly Hogan, Nora O'Connor, Scott Ligon, Casey McDonough, Alex Hall They played two hour-long sets, ending the first and beginning the second with the same Chris Ligon song.  Great sound, super harmonies, some choice covers, and as usual with Hogan projects the banter is as good as the music. Mastering: Audacity (compress to -6dB, normalize) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastered by: Brandon Media #0310 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.description: Reggie Watts July 11, 2015 Tour de Fat, Palmer Square Chicago IL Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Setlist: (76:40) 01. Michael Craft intros 02. on Chicago 03. my lady song 04. on beers 05. Christmas song 06. on frisbie 07. Chicago song 08. on schools 09. GPS song 10. bra song 11. bicycle song 12. on acronyms 13. hands song 14. outro announcements Song titles are all unofficial - Reggie is an improviser.  Tour de Fat is an "annual traveling bikes and beer celebration," and in 2013 it was at Chicago's Palmer Square on a Saturday.  Reggie Watts and Sister Sparrow & The Dirty Birds performed after a five-mile bike parade, and there was much dancing and fundraising. Mastering: Audacity (no levels adjustment, just compress to -0.2dB) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastered by: Brandon Media #0268 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Erin McKeown
items.description: Erin McKeown December 15, 2013 Hideout Chicago Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive ** 16 BIT ** Mastering: .FLAC > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .WAV > Izotope RX 11 [spectral de-noise, de-hum, normalize, fades] > CDWav [tracking] > Traders Little Helper V2.8.4 (Build 185) > .FLAC > MP3tag v3.28 [tagging]. Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: Popmarter Setlist: (96:05) Set 1 Erin McKeown and Susan Werner 01 Didn't They? 02 Santa Cruz 03 The Politician 04 The Jailer 05 Histories 06 You, Sailor 07 The Lions 08 A Better Wife 09 Track 09 (...melanie...) 10 Vera Set 2 Erin McKeown and The Cranky Carolers (Anti-Holiday Spectacular) 11 Intro 12 You Wish Us A Happy Holidays 13 Fa La La La (diffferent lyrics) 14 Santa Is An Asshole 15 Visions I Have Had While High 16 Anti-Holiday Aphorismes 17 Christmas Love It Or Leave It 18 Itsa Very Queer Christmas 19 Frozen Smiles 20 Introductions from The Cranky Carolers 21 Go Tell It On The Mountain (That Karl Rove Is Born) 22 Christmas Waltz Encore (Erin McKeown solo) 23 Toyland Tired of holiday hype? Gritting your teeth until New Year's Eve? Erin McKeown is too. Filled with profanity, irreverence, and just plain bad behavior, Erin McKeown's Anti-Holiday Spectacular is the antidote to all that annoying holiday spirit. It's the world's first anti-capitalist, pro-queer, suspicious of christmas-as-patriotism, sex-positive, not safe for work, multi-ethnic, radical leftist Anti-Holiday Show. The night will consist of two sets. In the first set, Erin performs a solo set of selections from all 10 of her albums. The second set is the Anti-Holiday Spectacular!! Erin will be joined onstage by a large, chorus of crowd-sourced Cranky Carolers. Please note this show contains adult language and themes completely inappropriate for children. On purpose. Medianumber 263 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project Coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.description: Moxie Tung Hideout Chicago, IL August 31, 2015 Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive ** 16 BIT ** Mastering: WAV > iZotope RX11 Standard v11.2.0.4231 (de-click to reduce close clapping) > Sound Studio 4.10.1 (very light dynamic compression, normalize, tracking) > WAV > xACT (fix SBEs, generate ffp) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: neil d Setlist: (08:52) 01 unknown (cuts in) ("they must be stopped in their tracks") 02 unknown ("the red ribbons of treachery") 03 unknown ("dungeons of the usa") Moxie Tung, a project of Sally Timms of the Mekons and Janet Bean of Freakwater and Eleventh Dream Day, has often been described as indescribable, and this rare fragment of a recording of one of their rare live shows shows this to be entirely accurate. Opened for Bad Luck Jonathan, who are only marginally more describable themselves. items.creator: Hawley Shoffner
items.description: Hawley Shoffner (as Bunny) Quenchers Saloon Chicago, IL May 1, 2015 Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive ** 16 BIT ** Source:  Crown Pressure Zone Microphone > Marantz PMD661 > .WAV @ 16 bit/44.1 kHz Mastering: WAV > iZotope RX11 Standard v11.2.0.4231 (de-click to reduce close clapping) > Sound Studio 4.10.1 (amplify/volume adjust to fix fluctuation gain at start of first song, very light dynamic compression, normalize, tracking) > WAV > xACT (fix SBEs, generate ffp) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transferred by: Avery Mastered by: neil d Setlist: (22:00) 01 intro 02 monologue 03 unknown (scared to grow older) 04 monologue 05 unknown (no clothes removed) 06 monologue 07 Big Old Bloody Thing 08 unknown (hold on to your feelings) From Britt Julious of the Chicago Tribune: “This past winter she created Bunny, a side project/alter-ego. Shoffner began writing songs in the character of Bunny, eventually finishing 15 monologues and a storyline that includes a signature pink wig and a personality imbued with naivete. Based on Candy Darling, an Andy Warhol Superstar and a muse for The Velvet Underground, Shoffner says her character is also an exploration of songwriting processes that differ from her normal routines. ‘It’s just easier sometimes to picture your life in a different way and just to write songs for someone else,’ she said. Despite the alter ego, Shoffner makes a point of identifying these alter egos as all her. ‘I think it’s important to do some stuff under my actual name for a second.’” Recording #0164 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Various Artists
items.description: VARIOUS ARTISTS  Saturday, 29 June 2013 The Green Mill Cocktail Lounge 4802 N. Broadway Chicago, Illinois  60640 USA Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive ** 16 BIT ** Mastering:  .WAV > shntool (joined WAVs) > Cool Edit Pro 2.0 (dynamic compression; minor declicking; normalization; added fades at beginning and end to both parts) > CDWav (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 8) > FLAC >  TagScanner 6.1.18 (tagging) Recorded by:  Aadam Jacobs Transferred by:  Avery Mastered by:  elegymart Total running time [1:12:38] -------------------------------------------------------- 01  Christopher Piatt - Introduction [2:19] 02  Katie Rich and Steve Waltien - Tribute to American History [10:12] 03  Robbie Fulks - Where I Fell [4:15] 04  Robbie Fulks - Living in the U.S.A. [4:05] 05  Chad the Bird (Josh Zagoren) - Original Essay on American Mythology [8:36] 06  Adam Burke - Fourth of July International Perspective [7:30] = 07  Abe Peck - Government Surveillance [11:08] 08  John Hartman and Mike Kosinski from Second City - The Gay Guys from the Pride Parade [5:42] 09  Kim Bellware - Immigration [8:38] 10  Dan Granata - The Landlord's Game [10:09] Notes: https://www.uptownupdate.com/2013/06/special-star-studded-edition-of-paper.html?m=1 Paper Machete, the live radio magazine and revue show that airs each week from the Green Mill (4804 N Broadway) has a special show this weekend and would love for Updaters to be in the audience:   "We're taping our first-ever hour-long special for WBEZ. (It's an all-American special set to air on 4th of July.) We have big primo talent involved. Country music legend Robbie Fulks is playing music; plus the famous Rolling Stone journalist Abe Peck; Katie Rich and Steve Waltien from the Second City main stage; the Huffington Post's Kim Bellware; and the popular stand-up Adam Burke.   It should be a dynamite show, brimming with Chicago's best comic and literary talent. We'd love to get the word out, as we're hoping to pack the house since we're recording it for our public-radio debut." It all happens at 3pm this Saturday -- and every Saturday -- at the Green Mill, and it's always free.  Media 0148 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Reggie Watts
items.description: Reggie Watts July 13, 2013 Tour de Fat at Palmer Square Chicago, IL Recording generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Mastering: Audacity (no levels adjustment, just compress to -0.2dB) > FLAC Recorded by: Aadam Jacobs Transfer by: Avery Mastered by: Brandon 15 tracks (62:46) Song titles are all unofficial - Reggie is an improviser.  Tour de Fat is an "annual traveling bikes and beer celebration," and in 2013 it was at Chicago's Palmer Square on a Saturday.  Mucca Pazza and Reggie Watts performed after a five-mile bike parade, and there was much dancing and fundraising. Tape 0132 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.description: Scruffy The Cat September 30, 1988 The Cubby Bear Chicago, IL ** 24 BIT ** Master Cassette generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive Source:  Unknown mic & recorder > Cassette (M) Transfer:  Cassette (M) > Akai HX-R40 > Sony PCM-A10 (.WAV @ 24/48) Mastering:  .WAV's > iZotope RX11 Advanced v11.0.1.3871 (De-Click) > Sound Forge Pro 14.0 Build 140 [splicing, minor edits, normalize, & fades, Boz Digital Labs T-Bone2 plug-in (tilt EQ)] >  CDWav (tracking) > Trader's Little Helper (level 5) > FLAC > TagScanner 6.1.17 (tagging) Recorded by:  Aadam Jacobs Transferred by:  vanark Mastered by:  Dennis Orr Setlist: (1:46:27) 01 Crowd/Intro 02 Mybabyshe'sallright 03 C'mon Everybody 04 Thomas Doubter 05 Momma Killed Hate 06 Chat & Tuning 07 Route 66 08 Capital Moonlight 09 Shadow Boy 10 Tiger, Tiger 11 Bus Named Desire 12 Time Never Forgets 13 Blue Russian 14 You Dirty Rat 15 Chat & Tuning 16 Hip Hug-Her 17 Love So Amazing (fades in) 18 Kissing Galaxy 19 Carol 20 One Bad Apple 21 Chat & Tuning 22 Betty Drops In 23 Buy A Car > 24 Moons Of Jupiter 25 Never, Never 26 Nova SS 1968 27 Memphis, Tennessee 28 Happiness To Go 29 Life Is Fun 30 Encore Break #1 - Encore #1 - 31 Little Bit O' Soul > 32 Upside Down 33 Encore Break #2 - Encore #2 - 34 I Got You (I Feel Good) 35 I Saw Her Standing There 36 Somethin' Else 37 Reelin' & Rockin' > 38 Matchbox 39 Running Bear 40 Outro Charlie Chesterman - lead vocals, guitar, & harmonica Stephen Fredette - lead guitar & vocals Burns Stanfield - keyboards Mac Stanfield - bass Randall Lee Gibson IV - drums Mastering Notes: Tape flip after "Hip Hug-Her" - seamlessly spliced, the first few seconds of "Love So Amazing" are missing as the song fades in The recorder was paused and re-started during the "Encore Break" applause - no music was lost  Tape flip during the "Encore Break #2" applause - seamlessly spliced, no music was lost Note: Master Cassette generously loaned from the Aadam Jacobs Audio Archive. Tape 0003 from the Aadam Jacobs Collection Project, coordinated by the Live Music Archive at the Internet Archive Contact info: aadam.jacobs.project AT gmail DOT com

items.creator: Neil Innes and Friends
items.description: Neil Innes and Friends Martyr's, Chicago, IL 2004-04-21 Neil Innes & Ken Thornton 01 Intro 02 How Sweet to be an Idiot 03 I'm the Urban Spaceman 04 Comedy / Pathos / Short Blues 05 Live is Getting Deeper 06 intro 07 Evening Sun 08 Godfrey Daniel 09 Protest Song 10 Introducing Rutland Ken 11 Friends at the End 12 You're Never Alone 13 introduction 14 Ego Warriors Neil Innes -acoustic guitar, keyboard, harmonica, Martin backpacker, vocals Ken Thornton - electric guitar, tracks 11, 12 and 14 Neil Innes & Friends - full band set 01 Intro 02 One of Those People 03 Equestrian Statue 04 Randy Raquel 05 Eine KJleine Middle Klasse Musik 06 With a Girl Like You 07 Hey Mister 08 Doubleback Alley 09 Good Times Roll 10 Another Day 11 Cheese and Onions 12 Intro 13 I Want to Be With You 14 Philosopher's Song 15 Feel No Shame 16 Bold Sir Robin 17 Goose-Step Mama 18 I Must Be In Love 19 intro 20 Ouch! 21 Questionnaire 22 Piggy in the Middle 23 Joe Public 24 Get Up and Go 25 intro 26 I'm the Urban Spaceman Neil Innes - electro-acoustic guitar, keyboard, vocals Ken Thornton - electric guitar, guitar-synth, backing vocals Ken Simpson - acoustic guitar, backing vocals "Pink Bob" Harper - keyboards, claves, ukelele, underwater trumpet Clay Thompson - bass guitar, backing vocals John Ganser - drums Tom Sparrow - percussion Recorded to DAT by Aadam Jacobs.  Live sound mixing and stereo audio feed by Ted Dunham CD produced by Ken Thornton and Pink Bob.  Edited and mastered by Pink Bob; additional engineering by Ken Thornton. Additional notes/trivia from da9ve:  Pink Bob Harper ran the FOT and Ponk record labels out of Bloomingdale and Bloomington/Normal, Illinois, in the '80's and '90's - a label that produced the amazing "Passed Normal" compilations of abnormal music, both from semi-famous artists including David Thomas/Pere Ubu, Snakefinger, the Residents, Gong/Daevid Allen/Hugh Hopper, Chris Cutler, Peter Blegvad, and of relatively non-famous regional acts. https://www.discogs.com/label/27633-FOT-Records https://www.discogs.com/label/103944-PONK-Records https://www.discogs.com/label/553883-Passed-Normal Clay Thompson and John Ganser were 2/3 of the Illinois-based power-trio The Spelunkers, a criminally underrecognized band, who were in turn a spinoff of The Something Brothers, who now apparently have re-released their catalog via Bandcamp. https://www.discogs.com/artist/951321-Something-Brothers https://somethingbrothers.bandcamp.com/ https://www.discogs.com/artist/3202497-Spelunkers

The two identifiable songs are Green Eyes and Hate Paper Doll. There are bits and pieces that sound like other songs Track list: 01. [soundcheck] 02. [intro] 03. Something I Learned Today 04. items.creator: Hüsker Dü
items.description: Hüsker Dü — The West End, Chicago IL, 16 Jun 1984 (all ages early show) Audience recording by Aadam on Radio Shack Micro X11 cassette recorder with Beyer mic (model unknown) Master cassette ripped by Aadam: Nakamichi LX-5 -> wav file Raw wav file edited by plh in Audacity:  • Track IDs inserted  • Flagrant spikes attenuated manually  • Peak level amplified to 0dB  • No compression or EQ applied  • Tracks exported to FLAC, MP3 (320 kbps) Be advised that while this is a valuable document, it is not a great recording. It's an early effort by a future world-class taper learning his chops on rather primitive equipment. There is no companion late-show recording; the taper was too young to be admitted. Track list: 01. Something I Learned Today 02. It's Not Funny Anymore 03. From The Gut 04. Wheels 05. Everything Falls Apart 06. The Girl Who Lives On Heaven Hill 07. I Apologize 08. Books About UFOs 09. Folklore 10. If I Told You 11. Listen 12. Terms Of Psychic Warfare 13. Broken Home, Broken Heart 14. Diane 15. Masochism World 16. What Do I Want? 17. Pride 18. Statues->Reoccurring Dreams->Statues 19. [encore break + feedback] 20. Pink Turns To Blue 21. Out On A Limb 22. New Day Rising 23. [outro]
